popularStory.headline: Jean II le bon (1319-1364)
popularStory.description: Jean II, dit Jean le Bon, né le 26 avril 1319 au château du Gué de Maulny du Mans et mort à Londres le 8 avril 1364, fils du roi Philippe VI et de son épouse Jeanne de Bourgogne, est roi de France de …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France於1319年4月26日出生在法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易薩特勒 曼斯時，他的父親Philippe VI de Valois Roi de France25歲，母親Jeanne de Bourgogne Reine de France27歲。他於1332年7月28日與Bonne de Luxembourg在法國瑟-吉馬內默倫結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1364年4月6日在英國米德萊可倫敦享年44歲，埋葬在法國瑟沙吉登尼斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France于1319年4月26日出生在法国帕斯 德 拉 洛易薩特勒 曼斯时，他的父亲Philippe VI de Valois Roi de France25岁，母亲Jeanne de Bourgogne Reine de France27岁。他于1332年7月28日与Bonne de Luxembourg在法国瑟-吉馬內默倫结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他去世于1364年4月6日在英国米德萊可倫敦享年44岁，埋葬在法国瑟沙吉登尼斯。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: France
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: ethnic name for someone from France (see 2 below).

French: habitational name given to someone from France (at the time when the name only applied to those lands belonging to the French king, covering only a small part of its current territory) or from Île-de-France (i.e. from a region centered on Paris); or, in southern France, an ethnic name for someone from what is now the northern half of the country, where the langue d'oïl (Old French as distinct from Occitan) was spoken. Compare   Defrance   and   Lafrance  .
Czech (Franče): from a pet form of František, Latin Franciscus (see Francis ).

variants.name: Defrance
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Henri, Jean-Paul.

French (mainly northern): habitational name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, given to someone from France (at the time when the name only applied to those lands belonging to the French king, covering only a small part of its current territory) or from Île-de-France (i.e. from a region centered on Paris). Compare France .

nameMeanings.name: Jean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

nameMeanings.name: Bon"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Benjamin  , or less commonly of   Benedict   or   Bennett .  Since the mid-1980s it has also been very popular as an independent given name.

variants.name: Benjamin
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the youngest of the twelve sons of Jacob. His mother Rachel died in giving birth to him, and in her last moments she named him Benoni, meaning ‘son of my sorrow’. His father, however, did not wish him to bear such an ill-omened name, and renamed him Benyamin (Genesis 35:16–18; 42:4). This means either ‘son of the right hand’ or more likely ‘son of the south’ (Hebrew yamin can also mean ‘south’), since Benjamin was the only child of Jacob born in Canaan and not in Mesopotamia to the north. Another tradition is that the second element of the name is a variant of the Hebrew plural noun yamim, which means ‘days’ but is used idiomatically to mean ‘year’ or ‘years’. The name would then mean ‘son of (my) old age’ and refer to the fact that Benjamin was Jacob's youngest child. In the Middle Ages the name was often given to sons whose mothers had died in childbirth. Today it has no such unfortunate associations and it grew enormously in popularity following the release of the film The Graduate ( 1967 ), in which Dustin Hoffman played the role of Benjamin Braddock . It is used in Scotland as an Anglicized form of Gaelic Beathan .

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France was born on 26 April 1319, in Le Mans, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, France, his father, Philippe VI de Valois Roi de France, was 25 and his mother, Jeanne de Bourgogne Reine de France, was 27. He married Bonne de Luxembourg on 28 July 1332, in Melun, Seine-et-Marne, France. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 6 daughters. He died on 6 April 1364, in London, Middlesex, England, at the age of 44, and was buried in Saint-Denis, Seine, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France est né le 26 avril 1319, à Le Mans, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, France, son père, Philippe VI de Valois Roi de France, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Jeanne de Bourgogne Reine de France, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Bonne de Luxembourg le 28 juillet 1332, à Melun, Seine-et-Marne, France. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé le 6 avril 1364, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterré à Saint-Denis, Seine, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France am 26. April 1319 in Le Mans, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Philippe VI de Valois Roi de France 25 und seine Mutter Jeanne de Bourgogne Reine de France 27. Er hat am 28. Juli 1332 in Melun, Seine-et-Marne, Frankreich Bonne de Luxembourg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb am 6. April 1364 im Alter von 44 Jahren in London, Middlesex, England und wurde in Saint-Denis, Seine, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France è nato il il 26 aprile 1319 nel Le Mans, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Francia, il suo padre, Philippe VI de Valois Roi de France, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Jeanne de Bourgogne Reine de France, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Bonne de Luxembourg il 28 luglio 1332, nel Melun, Seine-et-Marne, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 6 aprile 1364, nel London, Middlesex, England, all'età di 44 anni, e fu sepolto a Saint-Denis, Seine, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de Franceが1319年4月26日にフランスペイズ ド ラ ロイアーサルスル マンスで生まれた時，父Philippe VI de Valois Roi de Franceは25歳，母Jeanne de Bourgogne Reine de Franceは27歳であった。1332年7月28日にフランスセアイネ-エト-マルンメルンでBonne de Luxembourgと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1364年4月6日にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンで44歳で死去し，フランスセアインサイント-デニスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France(이)가 1319년 4월 26일 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 사르테 일 먼스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Philippe VI de Valois Roi de France(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Jeanne de Bourgogne Reine de France(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1332년 7월 28일 프랑스 세이인-트-마니 멜루에서 Bonne de Luxembourg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1364년 4월 6일 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 세이인 세인트-데니스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France nasceu em 26 de abril de 1319, em Le Mans, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, França, seu pai, Philippe VI de Valois Roi de France, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Jeanne de Bourgogne Reine de France, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Bonne de Luxembourg em 28 de julho de 1332, em Melun, Seine-et-Marne, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele faleceu em 6 de abril de 1364, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, com 44 anos, e foi sepultado em Saint-Denis, Seine, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France родился 26 апреля 1319 г. в местности л манс, сарт, пеис де ла лоир, Франция, его отцу по имени Philippe VI de Valois Roi de France было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Jeanne de Bourgogne Reine de France, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Bonne de Luxembourg 28 июля 1332 г. в местности мелун, сине-ет-марн, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 6 дочерей. Он умер 6 апреля 1364 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия в возрасте 44 лет и был похоронен в местности сеинт-денис, син, Франция.
